dynamic_line.//~: Ahh, now that's a big question.  Not something I'd share with anyone who wandered in.  But yes, I know, you have one of our interfaces.  You carry our trust with you, and maybe you have the right to know.
dynamic_line.str: Ahh, now that be a weighty ask.  Not a thing ol' Rubik shares wi' every soul as wanders in, mind.  But aye, I know it, clear as day - that ye've the interface set in ye proper.  Ye carry the work in ye own flesh, so mayhaps ye've the right o' it, fair an square.

responses.text: I'm glad you trust me.  Tell me more.

dynamic_line.//~: Listen carefully:  not everything we gather comes from places like this.  Sometimes, we find places we call "labyrinths."  Stairs down to other worlds.  We keep track of them.  And, one is nearby, spotted by Benzete themselves.  These places aren't safe, and they aren't friendly.  But, they seem made for us, which is convenient, if you don't think too hard about it.  Honestly, it's where most of our bionics come from.
dynamic_line.str: Hear it true:  not all we gather is from these otherlands, and otherlands like it.  At times, we happen by places this'n might call "labyrinths."   Apples an' pears down to otherlands, in their own right.  The Exodii keeps the reckonin'.  An there's one "nearby", so t'speak, spotted by Benzete 'emself.  These places have no safe ground, an nothin' there be friendly.  Yet the ones we find seem made fer us'n.  Right convenient, if ye don't press it o'er hard.  Truth be, it's where most'o'our bright an' steelies comes from.

responses.text: So these "labyrinths" are like other worlds?  But not?  What ARE they?
How would I get to these "labyrinths?"

dynamic_line.//~: What are they, you ask?  If I knew, I'd be afraid to say.  They aren't like other places.  They don't just exist.  Something put them there, piece by piece.  A workshop of unknown purpose.  But also, not like any of that.  Honestly, not really sure if they exist before we pass by, or through them, anyway.  Anything we say about them is basically waxing religiously - maybe they can't be explained.
dynamic_line.str:
What are they, ye ask?  If ol' Rubil could name it clean, mayhaps I even wouldn'.  They ain't like other otherlands.  Not places what simply are.  Willed, these.  Set.  As if some vast mind laid'em out, piece by piece.  A labor-hall built for a purpose this'n don't ken."

Rubik pauses and scratches at their chin.

"But also… well, not like any o' that, neither.  Truth be, not rightly sure they're there before we pass by, or through 'em, any road.  By and by, what us'n says o' such places is waxin' religious, more than tellin'.  Mayhap they can't be known proper.

responses.text: So, they seem like someone made them?  But also, not really?  What kinds of things do you find there?

dynamic_line.//~: You find all manner of things.  But, you walk in and there are things where you might not expect them to be.  Tools ready to use.  Bionics ready to take, like bait on a snare, too tempting.  Listen carefully: you won't find gifts there.  You are a thief in the night, stealing what you want.  You're a fish, and the treasures are hooks.
dynamic_line.str: Ye find all manner of things - treasures in troves.  But, ye trapse in, an' things where they oughtn't.  Tools laid ready.  Bright an' steelies waitin' like a snare, too fair.  Mark ol' Rubik well:  what ye find there ain't gifts.  Yer a tea leaf in the night, nickin' what you want and can.  Yer a fish, and the treasure's a hook.

responses.text: What makes these things?  Is it whatever is raising the dead?
So, how would I get to these "labyrinths?"

dynamic_line.//~: No, not The Enemy, but something like their wife (Rubik is trying to be funny).  If you go, you'll see.  It'll talk to you.  It knows what shape to take, or it just takes it, anyway.  For me, an old friend, in a Temple of ale I barely remember.  For others, other things.  But, if it starts talking to you in a familiar way?  It it offers you safety?  Leave, quick and quiet.  It's a lie.  Death by another name.
dynamic_line.str: No, not The Enemy, but might be their trouble n' strife.  If ye' go, just wait n' see.  It'll ask.  It knows what shape to wear, or it just takes it, anyhow.  For me, an ol' friend, in the Temple of ale this'n scarce remembers.  For others, other things.  But, if it starts answerin' ye in a way just for you?  If it offers ye' a safety you think this broken world don't have?  Best be on yer way, quick and quiet.  It's a lie.  Just death by another name.

responses.text: So, how would I get to these "labyrinths?"

dynamic_line.//~: Heh, just trying to be funny, when I shouldn't be.  A bad joke.  Nothing funny about it.  I meant only this: it isn't the Enemy the one that raises the dead here.  But, they're maybe similar in some way.  Not family, not part of the Enemy, but maybe an equal to it.  So don't take the joke for more than a joke.  It's not its partner, its wife, or its friend, because I doubt these things have such things.  Maybe just an illusion - a trick in the head.  But that's just my thinking - it's all unknown.
dynamic_line.str:
Heh, just ol' Rubik tryin t'be clever, where clever can't be called for.  A poor jest, at that.  Nothin' funny in it."

Rubik's tone becomes a bit more somber - more serious.  "I meant only this:  it's not The Enemy what walks among the dead, but mayhaps say she's cut from the same cloth.  Not kin, not part of it, but this'n reckons alike, yet in its own fashion.  So don't take the jest for more than it be.  Ain't really a wife, friend, of anything of the sort, 'cause there ain't any such thing.  Mayhaps all an illusion - a jape of the lump.  But that's just my thinkin'.  Mayhaps.

responses.text: Okay, so whatever lives there is some sort of interdimensional horror.  Or maybe not.  Great.  How would I get to these "labyrinths?"

dynamic_line.//~: Getting there is tricky, but easier than moving a castle.  They're less expensive and dangerous than a whole jump, which itself is curious.  Downstairs is a metal ring with a terminal - I wrote the messages, so you'll understand it.  We'll consider you a volunteer who wants to scout the place out.  It's good for both of us.  You're free to use it.  Keep in mind that the teleporter needs to recharge for 5 days between jumps.  Your first time will leave you dizzy, but it'll pass if you're tough.
dynamic_line.str:
Gettin' there a mite tricky, but not so tangled as jumpin' this whole castle.  They're less dear to go to an' fro, so t'speak, which is odd itself, I s'pose.

Down below, ye'll find a ring o' metal, wi' a terminal us'n has just set up.  Ol' Rubik set the words by hand, in preparation, so ye should ken it well enough.  Ye'll be a free - a volunteer to escolt it.  Good for you, good for we - more metal for you, and sell extras to we, aye?  Ye're free to go an' make use of it.  Keep mind 'at once you jump, the ring'll need a full five day rest afore it'll take ye again.

First time'll leave ya' addled, though, spin o' the boat race.  If ye've the grit, passes quick enough.

responses.text: So there's a terminal downstairs?  I can just stand on the metal ring and use the terminal, but only once every five days or so?  Sounds too easy.  Sure, thanks Rubik.

dynamic_line.//~: A word of caution: I can't tell you what you'll find.  They're always different.  Don't trust them.  Don't linger.  Use your head, think like an Exodii:  take what you can, but don't get tempted by treasure.
dynamic_line.str: Last word o' threat:  Can't know what ye'll find there.  They're ever a bit different for each'n'all.  Trust not.  Tarry not.  Use yer lump and think as'n Exodii:  take what ye can, but be not tempted by treasure.

responses.text: It's clear you've been there, Rubik.  What was it like, really?

dynamic_line.//~: For me?  Well, twisted halls of steel.  But there's another place.  It's a little like a pub I barely remember, if it ever existed.  Warm.  Wood.  Fire.  Like a Temple (this should be capitalized, as it's what it calls itself for Rubik) for the soul, if I still have one.  But I don't stay there or trust it.  These days, I don't even visit.  I have other things to do, like run this shop.
dynamic_line.str: Fer me?  Well, twisting halls of steel, like this place, or that.  But there's another place.  A mite like a rub-a-dub house ol' Rubik scarce remembers, if it ever were.  Warm.  Wood.  Fire.  The ol' Temple for the soul, if there's any yet left.  Yet I tarry not there, trust it not a hair.  In fact, these days, I jus' don't go.  Got other things to do, like run this skip n' hop.

responses.text: I'm not sure that clears it up, but thanks Rubik, I'll be careful.
